iteration_notes: Manual dead-row elimination (index before layernorm) gives 6.21us which BEATS the oracle. Confirms ALGEBRAIC_ELIMINATION: if Inductor pushes index[-1] backward through row-independent layernorm, gap closes entirely.

Inductor already generates 1 fused kernel. Gap is from generic reduction template vs dedicated 1-warp row kernel (HIDDEN=128). max_autotune narrowed gap to 1.4x in earlier run but was inconsistent. No config reliably closes the gap. True NEW_PATTERN: dedicated embedding+layernorm template needed.

Manual rewrite (normalize all rows, index last) gives 1 kernel at 8.42us (1.22x gap). With max_autotune+CD: 9.25us (1.34x). Remaining gap is that oracle only writes affine for 1 row while Inductor must materialize all 64 normalized rows. Two-level fix: (1) ALGEBRAIC_ELIMINATION to skip dead affine rows, (2) fused multi-output kernel that shares row stats between div_tensor and indexed affine output.
